fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity utah, how to transfer physical therapist license to utah, physical therapist compact utah, utah physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Utah DOPL — Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address: 160 East 300 South, Salt Lake City, UT 84111

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Utah.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Create an account on the Department of Commerce online licensing system and complete the online application for a Physical Therapist license. Pay the non-refundable application fee of $120.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying receipt of your physical therapy degree, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL).

steps.title: Submit NPTE Scores
steps.description: Ensure your official National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores are sent directly from the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) to DOPL.

steps.title: Pass Utah Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Complete and pass the Utah Physical Therapist/Physical Therapist Assistant Jurisprudence Examination, which is administered online through the DOPL system once your application is submitted.

steps.title: Await Licensure Review and Issuance
steps.description: Once all required documentation and examination results have been received and verified by DOPL, your application will be reviewed for final approval and your license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Utah have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Utah participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Utah without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Utah
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Utah, you must create an account and complete the application through the Department of Commerce online licensing system.

official_links.portal_name: Department of Commerce online licensing system
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Department of Commerce online licensing system. Click 'Register for an account' if you are a new user, then follow the prompts to create your account with a user ID and password. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License'.
